title: Rich Tender Vietnamese Beef Stew
description: A lowfat, slowbraised beef stew bursting with fragrant coriander, lemongrass, and tomato, perfect for a lazy Sunday.

ingredients.name: Beef chuck
ingredients.notes: cut into 2‑inch cubes, trim excess fat

instructions: Brown the beef cubes in a large pot over medium‑high heat, turning until all sides are caramelized. Remove the beef and set aside.
Sauté onion, carrot, and garlic in the same pot, stirring until fragrant and softened, about 3 minutes.
Add lemongrass, coriander stems, and tomato paste to the pot, cooking for another minute to release flavors.
Return the beef to the pot; pour in beef broth, soy sauce, fish sauce, brown sugar, and red pepper flakes. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, cover, and reduce heat to low. Cook for 1¾–2 hours, or until the beef is fall‑apart tender.
Taste the stew and adjust seasoning with salt and black pepper if desired. Discard the lemongrass stalk and any large stems of coriander.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh coriander leaves and a wedge of lime on the side.
If desired, add a splash of fresh lime juice before serving for extra brightness.
